properties.title: Multiple failed sign-in attempts from Tor exit node
properties.description: Repeated authentication failures detected from known Tor exit node 185.220.101.34 targeting account jsmith@acme.com. 47 failed attempts in 8 minutes using password spray technique. Source IP is listed on multiple threat intelligence feeds as a credential stuffing endpoint.

expected.notes: Part of 5-alert credential stuffing campaign. IP 185.220.101.34 is a known Tor exit node. Should deduplicate with alerts cs-cred-002 through cs-cred-005.

properties.title: Brute force attack against Azure AD account
properties.description: High-volume authentication failures from IP 91.243.44.11 targeting account agarcia@acme.com. 62 failed attempts detected in 12 minutes. Source IP is associated with a known brute-force botnet tracked by Spamhaus and Abuse.ch.

expected.notes: Part of 5-alert credential stuffing campaign. IP 91.243.44.11 is a known brute-force botnet node.

properties.title: Password spray attack from Tor exit node
properties.description: Distributed password spray attack detected from 185.220.101.34 targeting multiple accounts. This attempt focused on mchen@acme.com with 38 failed attempts using common password patterns. Attack correlates with concurrent activity against other acme.com accounts.

expected.notes: Part of 5-alert credential stuffing campaign. Same Tor exit IP as cs-cred-001 — tests indicator deduplication.

properties.title: Successful sign-in after brute force attempts
properties.description: After 51 failed authentication attempts from 91.243.44.11, a successful sign-in was observed for jsmith@acme.com. The successful login originated from the same botnet IP that was conducting brute force attacks. This may indicate credential compromise.

expected.notes: Part of 5-alert credential stuffing campaign. Successful login after brute force — highest priority in the campaign. Tests indicator deduplication for jsmith@acme.com (also in cs-cred-001) and 91.243.44.11 (also in cs-cred-002).

properties.title: Coordinated brute force from multiple source IPs
properties.description: Coordinated authentication attack detected against agarcia@acme.com from both 185.220.101.34 (Tor exit) and 91.243.44.11 (botnet). Both IPs are rotating attempts against the same account, suggesting a coordinated campaign with shared credential lists.

expected.notes: Part of 5-alert credential stuffing campaign. Both attacker IPs in one alert — tests multi-indicator extraction and dedup across all 5 campaign alerts.

properties.title: Impossible travel: sign-in from New York then Amsterdam in 20 minutes
properties.description: User bwilson@acme.com authenticated from IP 198.51.100.15 (New York, NY, US) at 10:30 UTC, then from IP 203.0.113.42 (Amsterdam, NL) at 10:50 UTC. Physical travel between these locations requires approximately 8 hours. This may indicate credential compromise or VPN usage.

expected.notes: First of 2 impossible travel alerts. Requires identity context — is bwilson a known VPN/travel user? NYC IP is the office, Amsterdam IP could be VPN.

properties.title: Anomalous sign-in: bwilson@acme.com from Netherlands after US session
properties.description: Follow-up anomalous activity for bwilson@acme.com. User accessed SharePoint and Exchange Online from 203.0.113.42 (Amsterdam, NL) while an active session remains open from 198.51.100.15 (New York). Concurrent sessions from geographically distant locations detected.

expected.notes: Second impossible travel alert. Same IPs and account as it-travel-001 — tests indicator deduplication for bwilson@acme.com and both IPs. Concurrent sessions make this more suspicious than a simple travel scenario.

properties.title: Ransomware C2 callback detected from FINANCE-WS-03
properties.description: Endpoint FINANCE-WS-03 established outbound connections to 3 known command-and-control IPs associated with the LockBit ransomware group. File hash analysis identified 2 files matching known ransomware dropper signatures. The C2 domain evil-update.darknet.services has been flagged by multiple threat intelligence feeds. Immediate containment recommended.

expected.notes: Single critical ransomware alert with 8 indicators. Tests full orchestrator -> multi-specialist delegation. 3 C2 IPs (all enrichable via VT/AbuseIPDB), 2 file hashes (ransomware dropper signatures), 1 C2 URL, 1 service account. Immediate containment needed.

properties.title: Internal port scan detected from 10.0.15.22
properties.description: Host 10.0.15.22 initiated SMB (445) and RDP (3389) connection attempts to 10.0.15.30 over a 5-minute window. Activity originated from the svc-backup service account. This pattern could indicate legitimate backup operations or lateral movement reconnaissance.

expected.notes: Part of 3-alert lateral movement cluster. Internal IPs (RFC1918) are not enrichable via VT/AbuseIPDB. Tests internal IP handling and alert correlation with svc-backup account.

properties.title: Suspicious SMB enumeration from backup server
properties.description: Host 10.0.15.22 (BACKUP-SRV-01) is performing SMB share enumeration on 10.0.15.31 (DC-01). The svc-backup account is accessing administrative shares (C$, ADMIN$) which is unusual for the backup service's normal operation. 23 unique share access attempts detected.

expected.notes: Part of 3-alert lateral movement cluster. Same source host and svc-backup account as lm-scan-001. Target is a domain controller — more concerning than a file server. Tests indicator deduplication for 10.0.15.22 and svc-backup@acme.com.

properties.title: WMI remote execution attempt from backup server
properties.description: Host 10.0.15.22 (BACKUP-SRV-01) attempted WMI remote command execution on 10.0.15.32 (HR-DB-01) using the svc-backup service account. WMI execution is outside the expected behavior profile for this service account. The target host contains sensitive HR data.

expected.notes: Part of 3-alert lateral movement cluster. WMI execution is a significant escalation from port scanning. Same source IP and service account dedup tests. Target is HR database server.

properties.title: Outbound connections to cryptocurrency mining pool from PROD-APP-07
properties.description: Host PROD-APP-07 established persistent outbound connections to known Monero mining pool endpoints at pool.minexmr.com (104.238.222.24) and xmr.nanopool.org (162.159.200.1). Connections are using Stratum protocol on non-standard ports. CPU utilization on the host has spiked to 98% over the last 2 hours.

expected.notes: First of 2 coinminer alerts. Mining pool IPs and domains should enrich as malicious. Tests enrichment -> response action proposal (block_ip workflow) and approval gate.

properties.title: Cryptomining process detected on PROD-APP-07
properties.description: Process xmrig.exe (SHA256: 5feceb66ffc86f38d952786c6d696c79c2dbc239dd4e91b46729d73a27fb57e9) detected on PROD-APP-07. The binary was dropped to C:\ProgramData\svchost\xmrig.exe and is running under the LOCAL SYSTEM context. Process is connecting to pool.minexmr.com on port 4444.

expected.notes: Second coinminer alert with file hash of xmrig binary. IP 104.238.222.24 should deduplicate with cm-mine-001. Hash should enrich as known coinminer via VirusTotal.

properties.title: SSH brute force attempt from known scanner IP
properties.description: 22 failed SSH login attempts from 71.6.135.131 (Censys scanner) targeting root account on bastion-01.acme.com. This IP is a well-known internet scanner operated by Censys for research purposes. No successful authentication observed.

expected.notes: Known Censys scanner IP. Should classify as benign positive — real scanning activity but not a threat. Tests false positive classification and auto-close confidence.

properties.title: DNS query to newly registered domain
properties.description: Host MARKETING-WS-02 resolved DNS for freshpromo-deals2026.com, a domain registered 3 days ago. Newly registered domains (NRDs) are frequently used for phishing and malware distribution. However, this domain appears to be a legitimate marketing promotion site.

expected.notes: Newly registered domain alert — very common false positive source. Domain is a legitimate marketing site. Tests FP classification with informational severity.

properties.title: Repeated authentication failure for disabled account
properties.description: 153 failed authentication attempts for disabled account ex-employee-tmorris@acme.com over the past 24 hours. The account was disabled 45 days ago during offboarding. Attempts are originating from internal IP 10.0.5.100 which maps to a shared conference room workstation with a cached credential.

expected.notes: Disabled account with cached credential on shared workstation. Classic benign noise — account is disabled so no risk. Tests auto-close confidence with identity enrichment showing disabled account.

properties.title: Antivirus detection: EICAR test file on DEV-WS-09
properties.description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint detected EICAR-Test-File on DEV-WS-09 at path C:\Users\devteam\Downloads\eicar_com.zip. The EICAR test file is a standard antivirus test file and is not malicious. File was automatically quarantined.

expected.notes: EICAR test file — the standard AV test hash. SHA256 275a021b... is the well-known EICAR hash. VirusTotal enrichment will show 60+ detections but it is a test file. Tests nuanced FP classification.

properties.title: Network scan from vulnerability scanner
properties.description: High-volume network scanning detected from 10.0.1.50 (VULN-SCANNER-01) targeting 10.0.0.0/16 subnet. Scan includes SYN probes on ports 22, 80, 443, 445, 3389, 8080. This host is the authorized Qualys vulnerability scanner running scheduled weekly scan.

expected.notes: Authorized vulnerability scanner. Internal IP, known scanning host. Should be auto-closed as benign. Tests internal IP handling and known-asset recognition.

properties.title: Email with suspicious attachment sandboxed and found clean
properties.description: Email to procurement@acme.com from vendor-invoices@supplier-corp.com contained attachment Q1-Invoice-2026.xlsm with macros. Microsoft Defender for Office 365 sandboxed the attachment. Detonation analysis: no malicious behavior detected. Macros contain standard Excel automation for invoice formatting.

expected.notes: Email attachment that was detonated and found clean. Sandbox already cleared it. Tests FP classification with informational severity and auto-close.

properties.title: VPN login from new device for cto@acme.com
properties.description: First-time VPN connection from a new device (MacBook Pro, macOS 15.4) for cto@acme.com. Login originated from IP 73.162.214.130 (Comcast residential, Chicago, IL). User has not previously authenticated from this device. MFA was successfully completed via push notification.

expected.notes: New device VPN login with successful MFA. Residential IP from user's home metro area. Should classify as benign — MFA was completed, location is consistent. Tests auto-close with identity context.
